title: Maternal use of methamphetamine induces sex-dependent changes in myocardial gene expression in adult offspring.
ehr_evidence: None. The study is an experimental laboratory study using rat models.
summary: This study investigates the effects of prenatal methamphetamine exposure on myocardial gene expression in adult offspring, specifically examining sex-dependent changes in cardiac gene expression.
key_findings: Prenatal methamphetamine exposure induces sex-dependent changes in myocardial gene expression in adult offspring.
Female offspring exhibited 184 changes in gene expression compared to 74 in males.
Dimethylarginine dimethylaminohydrolase 2 and 3-hydroxybutyrate dehydrogenase 1 were under-expressed in females prenatally exposed to methamphetamine.
